title: WASiM Model
description: Canonical model definition for a WASiM probabilistic dynamic simulation.

wasim_version.description: WASiM schema version this file conforms to (semver).

source.description: Provenance metadata. Not consumed by the engine.

seed.description: RNG seed for reproducibility. Null for random.

elements.description: Flat list of all model elements. Hierarchy is expressed via container references.

time_history_displays.description: Display-only expressions from TimeHistoryResult plots in the source model. Not part of the simulation graph; preserved for inspection. Accepts the same provenance fields as element_base (source_type, goldsim_object_id, path, input_ports).

quantity.description: A numeric value with explicit unit declaration. The value is expressed in the declared unit — not necessarily SI base units. The engine converts to SI at load time for internal computation. display_unit controls how the value is presented to users in the frontend (defaults to unit if absent).

value.description: Numeric magnitude in the declared unit.

unit.description: Unit of value (e.g. 'mm/yr', 'kg/m3', 'd', '1' for dimensionless). May be any physically meaningful unit string; the engine is responsible for normalising to SI at load time.

display_unit.description: Preferred unit for frontend display and user input. If null, defaults to unit. The engine ignores this field.

quantity_expr.description: A value that may be either a fixed quantity or an expression AST.

quantity_or_formula.description: A distribution parameter that may be a fixed quantity, a parsed expression AST, or a formula string referencing other model elements. Prefer the expression_field (AST) form when the transpiler can parse the formula — engines should consume the AST directly. The string form is a fallback for unparsed formulas; engines that don't carry their own parser should treat unparsed strings as 0.0 and emit a warning.

oneOf.description: Unparsed formula expression (fallback when transpiler parse failed).

parent.description: ID of parent container, or null for top-level.

source_type.description: Original element kind in the source modelling tool (e.g. 'Container', 'Group'). Engine ignores.

goldsim_object_id.description: Original GoldSim object ID for round-tripping. Engine ignores.

path.description: Full container path in the source model. Engine ignores.

ast_node.description: A single node in a WASiM expression AST. Discriminated by 'op'.

oneOf.description: Numeric literal with optional unit.
Reference to another element's output.
Reference to a simulation time property.
Binary arithmetic or logical operation.
Unary operation.
Built-in scalar function call.
Inline ordered array constructed from scalar expressions. Used for vector/array operations. Equivalent to a GoldSim 'vector(a, b, c)' call.
If/then/else conditional.
Evaluate a lookup-table element at a given input. The element_id must refer to an element of type 'lookup'. The engine interpolates into that table using the evaluated input.

element_id.description: ID of the lookup element to evaluate.

input.description: Expression for the lookup's independent variable.

input2.description: Second independent variable for 2D lookup tables (optional).

expression_field.description: Reusable bundle of AST + optional display string.

display.description: Human-readable expression string. Not consumed by the engine.

source.description: How the expression was recovered. 'explicit' = decoded directly from the source binary (formula text or token table). 'inferred' = legacy generic inference (kept for backward compatibility). 'inferred_path' = port-path reference like '\Container\Element' resolved to a passthrough. 'inferred_passthrough' = single-incoming-connection passthrough. 'inferred_container' = container-boundary passthrough. 'inferred_ts' = name-convention TimeSeries passthrough (e.g. Precip → Precip_TS). 'inferred_pool_rate' = pool rate AST built from inflow/request port bindings rather than inline formula. 'inferred_ast' = compiled-AST node graph (no formula text) decoded into structured AST nodes; current Phase-1 implementation handles single-IDSNode passthrough refs. 'inferred_stub' = formula extraction failed (typically Selectors with compiled-AST conditions); placeholder Literal(0.0) emitted with connection-derived inputs to preserve graph topology. Engines should treat these as known-incorrect and warn.

process_spec.description: Parameters for a stochastic process element. Values are in user-chosen units; the engine scales drift linearly with dt and stddev by √(dt/T_ref) at each timestep.

family.description: 'gbm' = geometric Brownian motion: lognormal per-step increments, no memory between steps.

mean_type.description: Interpretation of the 'mean' parameter. 'geometric': mean is the per-period CAGR as a fraction (e.g. 0.07 for 7%/yr); engine derives log_drift = ln(1 + mean). 'arithmetic': mean is the arithmetic expected return; engine applies Itô correction (log_drift = ln(1+mean) - σ²/2). 'log_drift': mean is the log-space drift directly, no transformation.

mean.description: Central tendency of the process. Interpreted according to mean_type. Use the same unit as the intended output (e.g. '1/yr' for an annual return rate).

stddev.description: Per-reference-period volatility. Engine scales to per-step diffusion as σ_step = stddev.value × √(dt / T_ref) where T_ref is the period implied by stddev.unit. Annual volatility of 15% → stddev = {value: 0.15, unit: '1/yr'}.

correlation_group.description: Named correlation group for rank-correlation sampling.

oneOf.description: Bernoulli distribution (GoldSim 'Boolean'): 1 with probability p, 0 otherwise.
General discrete distribution with explicit probability table.

outcomes.description: Outcome values (e.g. 1.0, 2.0, … 6.0 for a six-sided die).

probabilities.description: Probability of each outcome. Must be same length as outcomes and sum to 1.

element_base.description: Fields common to all element types.

id.description: Unique identifier within the model. Used for cross-references.

container.description: ID of the containing container, or null for top-level.

source_type.description: Original element type from the source modelling tool (e.g. 'Pool', 'Integrator', 'Reservoir', 'Stochastic'). Preserves source-side semantics that may collapse to the same wasim type. Engine ignores.

goldsim_object_id.description: Original GoldSim object ID for round-tripping to .gsm. Engine ignores.

path.description: Full container path in the source model (e.g. '\System_Model\Tailings\Reclaim_from_Tailings'). Engine ignores; preserved for diagram rendering and cross-referencing port-path passthroughs.

input_ports.description: Richer connection metadata alongside 'inputs[]'. Each entry names a source element and, when known, the specific output port on the source and input port on this element. Engine consumes 'inputs[]'; this field is provenance for renderers/round-tripping.

output.description: Name of the upstream element's output port that feeds this connection, if known.

input.description: Name of this element's input port that the connection terminates on, if known.

oneOf.description: Fixed scalar value, optionally editable at runtime.

editable.description: If true, value can be adjusted by the user at runtime without editing model.json.

oneOf.description: Constant 1-D array of scalar values, indexed positionally (1..N by convention). All values share a single unit. Consumers (e.g. vinterp / interp_array) operate on the values vector directly.

values.description: Array values in declared unit. Empty array indicates the values are not yet extracted from the source binary (see 'provenance').

provenance.description: Origin of the values vector. 'extraction_pending' means the type was inferred from consumer usage but the binary array body has not been decoded; consumers should treat values as unavailable.

oneOf.description: Samples from a probability distribution each realization. Value is constant within a realization.

autocorrelation.description: Lag-1 autocorrelation coefficient (0–1). Applied across simulation time steps within a realization.

trigger.description: When the random variable resamples per-timestep on a specific trigger (e.g. GoldSim Resample='On Changed', Trigger=EDay), this captures the trigger source. Engine treats per-step resampling identically regardless of trigger; this is provenance only.

source.description: Name or kind of the trigger that fires resampling. Common values: 'EDay' (elapsed-day index), 'Timestep'.

correlations.description: Cross-correlations with other random variables in the model.

oneOf.description: Re-draws from a stochastic process at every timestep. Each MC path gets independent draws at each step, modeling sequence-of-returns risk. Unlike random_variable (which draws once per path), variance of cumulative outcomes grows with horizon. Engine scales stddev by √(dt/T_ref) automatically; drift scales linearly.

lower_bound.description: Optional floor clamped on the per-step drawn value, expressed as a quantity in the same unit as process.mean. null = no bound. For GBM, lognormal increments are naturally bounded below by -1 (total loss); set lower_bound only to impose a tighter floor or when using a non-lognormal family.

oneOf.description: Computes a value from other elements via a mathematical expression.

inputs.description: Element IDs referenced by this expression. Redundant with the AST; used for fast dependency graph construction.

oneOf.description: Integrates a net rate over time. State variable with initial condition.

initial_expression.description: Expression evaluated at t=0 to compute the initial state. When present, overrides initial_value. Used when the initial condition is computed from other elements (e.g. Init_* elements in GoldSim models).

rate.description: Net rate of change per timestep (d/dt of state). The transpiler is responsible for encoding pool inflows minus loss formulas as a single rate expression.

min_value.description: Lower bound enforced after each timestep. Default 0 (pools cannot go negative).

capacity.description: Upper bound on state value. Null for unbounded.

inputs.description: Element IDs referenced by the rate expression.

inflows.description: Element IDs that contribute mass/value to this accumulator (additive). Source-semantic decomposition of the rate; redundant with rate.ast but preserves GoldSim Pool semantics for tools that need them. Engine consumes rate.ast.

requests.description: Element IDs that draw mass/value from this accumulator subject to availability (subtractive). Pool-specific source semantic — paired with 'inflows[]'. Engine consumes rate.ast.

oneOf.description: Externally supplied time-varying input, interpolated between data points.

times.description: Time coordinates, monotonically increasing, in times_unit.

values.description: Values at each time coordinate, in values_unit. Must be same length as times.

oneOf.description: 1D table-based interpolation mapping an input to an output.

y.description: Dependent variable values (1-D lookup). Same length as x.

columns.description: Output columns for a 2-D lookup table. Each inner array has the same length as x. Use input2 to select the column (1-indexed).

extrapolation.description: Behavior when input is outside the x range.

oneOf.description: Outputs a time-lagged version of an input signal.

input.description: Element ID of the signal to delay.

initial.description: Value to return before the lag duration has elapsed.

oneOf.description: Procedural computation block for expressions requiring iteration or local variables.

source.description: Raw script source text (human-readable; engine uses expressions).

expressions.description: Transpiler-parsed AstNode expressions, one per evaluable output formula. Index 0 is the primary output. Log-message formulas are excluded. Absent when no formulas could be parsed.

variables.description: Local variable names in declaration order. Index 0 is the implicit result variable (self-reference). Absent for scripts using the alternate binary format.

procedural.description: True if the source script body contains procedural opcodes (control flow / multi-statement assignments) that the engine cannot faithfully execute under Tier 1. Engines should warn when evaluating procedural scripts; expression-only scripts (false) are correctly evaluable as a single formula.

oneOf.description: Named ordered array of scalar values, each computed from an expression. Supports GoldSim vector patterns where multiple related quantities are grouped and operated on together (sum, mean, indexing).

values_unit.description: Unit shared by all elements of the array. Use '1' for dimensionless.

expressions.description: Ordered list of scalar expressions that constitute the array elements.

labels.description: Optional human-readable label for each element. Must match length of expressions if present.

inputs.description: Element IDs referenced across all expressions.
